{"society_power": 5.0, "state_power": 4.5, "society_change": -1.0, "state_change": -0.5, "key_event": "Independence democracy then 1969 Barre military coup", "reasoning": "Independence created a nominal democratic state, but institutional dualism, a thin fiscal base, and irredentist military overextension kept state capacity moderate-to-weak, while the year-end coup began crushing electoral politics. Society weakened as clan norms were politicized into fragmented patronage machines, eroding the autonomous \"cage\" that had traditionally constrained the state."}
